name: Maultaschen (Swabian Pockets)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour, eggs, and salt in a bowl and mix to make a pasta dough.
2. Add a little water and knead until it has a firm but elastic consistency.
3. Melt the butter in a skillet and fry the bacon with onions until translucent.
4. Combine the bacon mixture with sausage meat.
5. Moisten the hard roll in water, press dry, and grind through a meat grinder (better than a food mill or processor).
6. Add the bacon mixture, cooked spinach, ground meat or smoked farm sausage, leftover roast, etc., to the grinding.
7. Fold in the eggs, parsley, and seasonings; mix together. The filling should be very spicy.
8. Roll out the dough into rectangular sheets on a floured board (about twice as wide as desired 'Maultaschen').
9. Place dabs of filling at evenly spaced 3-inch intervals along one side of the sheet.
10. Mix egg and canned milk in a bowl and apply to spaces, outer edge, and fold line.
11. Fold the plain half over to cover the filling.
12. Press down firmly on the spaces around the packets.
13. Use a pastry wheel or knife to separate into 3-inch square or diamond-shaped 'Maultaschen'.
14. Cook thoroughly in beef stock or boiling salted water for about 10 to 15 minutes, depending on size.
15. Remove with a slotted spoon and allow to drain.
Serving suggestions:
- Cut onions into half-rings, fry in butter until golden brown, and empty over the 'Maultaschen'.
- Serve with potato salad or mixed green salad.
